mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when UNRATE falls below 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, which triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances during late-cycle periods, or the signal fails to predict returns when INDPRO growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3% signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the inflation threshold loses predictive accuracy when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market recovery, which sustains positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth falls below 1.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved earnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations following positive GDP prints, or the signal shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates severe supply-side weakness that depresses corporate profit expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal fails to predict returns when monetary policy is actively easing.
